figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

2.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: to say (used with great latitude)
properly, to mark (so as to be recognized), i.e. to remember; by implication, to mention;  to be male
physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)

to know (properly, to ascertain by seeing); used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially (including observation, care, recognition; and causatively, in
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an associated term), (often used adverb)
an exploit (of God), or a performance (of man, often in a bad sense); by implication, an opportunity
a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
to call out to (i.e. properly, address by name, but used in a wide variety of applications)
to be (causatively, make) lofty, especially inaccessible; by implication, safe, strong; used literally and figuratively
an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character

5.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: great (in any sense); hence, older; also insolent
